root_cause: The preview pipeline is DOM-clipped to the 1920x1080 composition, but OverlayEditor display bounds are not intersected with the composition. For cropped/mismatched-aspect clips, the render path also keeps object-fit: cover on the full off-screen element box, so the source content shown inside the visible box is computed from the oversized box rather than the visible composition intersection.

box_mismatch_example.scenario: Large uncropped image at x=658, y=0, width=2256, height=1692 on a 1920x1080 composition

discrepancy_preview_pixels.summary: Overlay extends 397.6px farther right and 244.8px farther down than the visible Remotion render at 0.4x preview scale.

content_mismatch_example.scenario: Cropped large image using T2 example: x=658, width=2256, height=1692, cropRight=0.53, cropBottom=0.53

discrepancy_source_pixels.note: The visible box is geometrically correct, but object-fit: cover shifts the source window because it centers the 3000x1000 image inside the full 2256x1692 off-screen box before clip-path runs.

A_viewport_intersection.why: Intersecting the render box with the composition and compensating with object-position reproduces the intended visible slice while giving OverlayEditor a matching display box. It is render-only and restores full size automatically when the clip comes back on-screen.

B_replace_object_cover.why: Explicit object-position/object-fit:none can remove the centering drift, but the oversized box would still need composition intersection for overlay/render agreement. Higher regression risk for normal in-bounds manual clips.

C_clamp_dimensions.why: Simple width/height clamping addresses the overflow geometry but throws away the hidden-source offset unless it grows into viewport-intersection math. On its own it is an incomplete version of A.

chosen_strategy.label: viewport-intersection
chosen_strategy.rationale: Strategy A is the only option that fixes both classes of discrepancy while honoring DECISION-006 and the render-only constraint. It lets the preview render only the visible intersection, applies the same intersection downstream in OverlayEditor display coordinates, and naturally restores the original dimensions when the user drags the clip back into view because the stored clip state is untouched.

browser_limit_verdict.note: All observed mismatches are explained by DOM/CSS layout math. The element sizes in the repro are far below typical browser canvas or texture limits, and the preview path does not use canvas for these images.

browser_attempt.note: Live screenshot/devtools validation remained blocked by sandboxed headless browser behavior. This report uses the actual overlay/render formulas plus source-inspection-based geometry to choose the fix strategy before code changes.
